campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's invested money performed and overall spending. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including ways to connect different types of transit like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City spending and budget review for July
Plans to improve transportation choices (buses, bikes, walking paths)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and decided to fast-track it by skipping the usual first reading process, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input and feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for quick adoption
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily focused on administrative approvals and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six different March meetings and sessions
Authorized a construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Administrative housekeeping and record approval

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The City Council held a planning meeting to organize upcoming agendas for late April. They reviewed what items should be added to the April 21st meeting, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked paperwork requirements. They also previewed the April 28th agenda and reviewed public records from early April.
keyTopics: Planning April 21 and April 28 council meeting agendas
Assigning time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewing legal notice requirements for agenda items
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion
Public records review for April 2-9 period

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including budgets for construction and infrastructure projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement projects and construction budget
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City infrastructure and spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to approve meeting notes from March and introduced a new rule to update tenant protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The changes aim to strengthen rules around how landlords treat residential tenants in the city.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to strengthen residential tenant protections
Amendment to Chapter 19.71 of city code regarding tenant rights

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also elected new leadership positions and discussed budget planning for the upcoming fiscal year, though the agenda item appears incomplete in the provided information.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on city matters
Election of new Council Chair and Vice Chair
Fiscal year budget development presentation
State law limits on decision-making on non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents about how long you have to challenge city decisions in court. If you disagree with a City decision, you have 90 days from the date the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit, unless state law says otherwise.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to legally challenge City decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 applies to Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, review new project requests from individual council members, and consider changing rules about how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan updates for housing and community development
New project requests from council members
Changes to how council handles vacant seats
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a historic building and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra space as part of renovations to make the building safer and preserve its historic character.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development approval
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
